last_result.effect: EasyCrypt accepted a proof-state change; the refreshed view is based on the new committed state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

moves.title: Proof-route context
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
moves.why_relevant: Name resolution can instantiate `pr_CCP_OCCP`, but the current Pr endpoint does not match the lemma's endpoint template; build an intermediate Pr equality or wrapper bridge first.

checks: Pr resources are context: use them when they match the visible endpoint and simplify the goal.
Direct byequiv is a valid route when the goal is naturally a program equivalence.

selected_handles.title: Proof-route context
selected_handles.why_relevant: Name resolution can instantiate `pr_CCP_OCCP`, but the current Pr endpoint does not match the lemma's endpoint template; build an intermediate Pr equality or wrapper bridge first.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: Some imported or cross-scope handles may need lookup if that route is being tested; use lookup_candidates for the concrete symbol instead of treating the whole list as a to-do queue.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Direct `apply`/`rewrite` does not match the raw endpoint yet, but this is a live Pr bridge checkpoint. If pursuing this Pr-bridge route, inspect it with `-where pr_CCP_OCCP` and probe a small intermediate Pr equality to one instantiated...
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Current Pr equality may need a manager-verified bridge route (game-hop or scheme/endpoint normalization) before direct byequiv/proc lowering.
ask_manager_for.returns: daemon-verified, committable Pr bridge routes with manager-owned intent payloads

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need pRHL/procedure-equivalence bridge lemma names or context after checking pr_bridge_routes.
ask_manager_for.returns: context-only equiv bridge lemma candidates (names to look up/apply); not a verified Pr route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need local lemma hints before choosing a Pr-level proof route.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and proof-route context

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need exact byphoare/phoare-loop tactic syntax before probing the probability route.

lookup_candidates.use_when: Resolve exact signature for `pr_CCP_OCCP` only when this handle route is being tested.

items.label: Before latest branch-local tactic #14
items.why_checkpoint: latest branch-local step inside the current seq scope
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #14 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.why_checkpoint: call-local boundary; selecting it keeps the call tactic and removes work committed after the call opened
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #13 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
